Subject: INVITATION TO THE 79TH IFALPA ANNUAL CONFERENCE
Body: Dear Andrew, On behalf of the IFALPA local organizing committee and its sponsors, we would like to officially invite you to participate in the 2025 Annual Conference, a key event for IFALPA member associations worldwide. This gathering provides a dedicated platform to strengthen collaboration and unity among pilots, fostering a collective approach to address the challenges facing the global aviation community. This landmark event will be held in Incheon, Republic of Korea from 3rd to 6th of April, 2025.. Special benefits from the local organizing executive committee and its sponsors: The executives committee and its sponsors have negotiated discount rates with major airline operators and hotel management, and they have accepted a 30% discount from our delegates if we are to make group bookings and hotel reservations for them. This is a first come, first-served basis. They sell out fast so book now.. This is first of its kind to support and assist our intended delegates with their trip. Important Information For those who require a visa to South Korea: Due to the new resolutions and recent decisions of the councilors in the immigration department regarding tourist visas to South Korea, due to global issues on terrorism and criminal tendencies of immigrants as this mostly affected those that are coming to South Korea on a tourist visa. This is entirely a new arrangement different from the old system just to check these global vices and also to discourage those without a genuine reason/intention from entering into South Korea. The local organizing executive committee therefore decided to assist the participants with their visa processing for those who require a visa to South Korea. The visa processing is free (no payment is required) and the processing time is 10 working days. We will process your visa here, and then email you a backup documents, and give you a lay-down on who to meet in our Embassy or Consulate in your home country with the command of the South Korean Immigration Bureau to stamp your visa with the document you will receive which will allows you to stay for a maximum of 60 days, it is important that we let you know the process. Delegates who wish to benefit from the 30% discount for the flight booking/hotel reservation and those who need assistance with their visa should email us immediately for further information and guidelines on how to benefit from this gesture. The 79th IFALPA Conference provides a valuable opportunity to stay informed, connect with peers, and contribute to discussions that shape the profession. We look forward to your participation! Warm regards, Capt. Amornvaj (Ben) Mansumitchai President- IFALPA

Subject: [spambayes-dev] I used SpamBayes for class project - thanks!
Body: Hi SpamBayes developers, I just wanted to let you know that I used SpamBayes for my term project in my Machine Learning class, and to say thanks. I thought there might be some academic interest here, and that I'd share with the list in return for the great code of yours that I used. The overall assignment was to "do something with evolutionary algorithms." The goal of my project was more or less to attack a spam filter and attempt to use an EA to find weaknesses it might have. I set up a blind watchmaker sort of natural selection/ evolution environment using a SpamBayes filter that I had trained on an old training corpus as the fitness function. The program I wrote attempted to evolve a generic formula that worked sort of like "MadLibs" to write spam emails that could get past the filter. I started off with almost zero knowledge of how a spam filter works, and tried to keep it that way while I wrote the algorithm so that it would be as naive as possible. I've since read up on SpamBayes and some of the math behind it, and it looks really interesting. I'm looking forward to taking more time to look through the codebase. Now that the project is completed, I see where I could have trained the filter on different content or parts of the evolution structure that I could have written differently to make this project more successful, but that's research for you! This was a very quick proof of concept project, and if I were to continue with this research there are certainly a lot of things I would do differently and many more complicated techniques that I'd like to try. If anyone is interested in reading about the project in more detail, I've posted the paper I wrote here: http://www.cs.brandeis.edu/~egifford/classes/egiffordTermProject.pdf Thanks again for maintaining this cool project. I hope you'll take my contribution in the intellectual sense that is intended, and not as an attack. :) Liz Gifford _______________________________________________ spambayes-dev mailing list bsfegmjrr-hnq@python.org http://mail.python.org/mailman/listinfo/spambayes-dev
